name: Justice Hill
headline: Pass-down change-up with a Henry-sized upside ceiling
body:
Hill's profile reads as a satellite back, not a workhorse. Across 10 games in 2025 he logged just 18 carries against 27 targets, hauling in 21 catches for 169 yards and a 6.6% target share, while his snap rate slid to 37.1%. The efficiency stayed real — 5.17 yards per carry, 3.89 yards after contact per attempt, and a 71-yard burst that flashed the breakaway speed Baltimore values — but a minus-0.019 EPA per play and only 44.2 fantasy points underline how thin the volume runs.

The 2024 tape is the tell: 51 targets, 42 grabs, three receiving scores and a plus-0.151 EPA per play when he actually got fed. With the primary backup role to Derrick Henry now his alone, Hill stays a deep-league passing-down piece on standalone value. The genuine appeal is contingency — if Henry misses time, this skill set converts into instant RB2 production overnight.
